content.problemStatement: Problem StatementWrite a program that accepts sets of three numbers, and prints the second-maximum number among the three.
constraints: The three integers in a single triplet are all distinct. That is, no two of them are equal.
content.inputFormat:
First line contains the number of triples, **N**.
The next **N** lines which follow each have three space separated integers.
content.outputFormat: For each of the **N** triples, output one new line which contains the second-maximum integer among the three.

solution.approach:
Since the three integers are distinct, sorting the list of three numbers and selecting the second element will directly give the second-largest number.
Sorting three numbers has constant time complexity `O(1)`, making this approach efficient.
solution.timeComplexity: Sorting the three numbers takes constant time O(1), since there are only three elements.

code.Python3:
n = int(input())  
for _ in range(n):
    nums = list(map(int, input().split()))  
    nums.sort()  
    print(nums[1])
